author: doll-agent
content:
Closed the multi-core gap iteration with two changes:

1. **Thread-local A-pack reuse**: replaced `vec![0.0; mc.div_ceil(MR)*MR*kc]` (allocated on every IC chunk per worker) with a `thread_local! { static A_PACK_TLS: RefCell<Vec<f64>> }` buffer that grows once per worker and is reused across all matmul calls. Each rayon worker (and the calling thread on the sequential path) keeps its own buffer; `with_a_pack(min_capacity, |buf| ...)` resizes lazily.

2. **SIMD pack_b**: vectorised the row-major B → NR-wide-strip packing to two unaligned 256-bit loads + stores per row (full strip case), runtime-gated on `is_x86_feature_detected!(avx2)`. Replaces 8 scalar copies per row.

3. Tried MC=32 to double IC chunks at moderate N — regressed at N>=320 (pack-call overhead doubled, marginal gain past 8 cores). Reverted to MC=64.

Stable performance at N=512 (median of 100 iters, 20 warmup, 2 batch runs):
- Before: 133 GFLOPs (148% of single-core peak)
- **After: 156 GFLOPs (174% of single-core peak)** — +17% multi-thread improvement

Progress vs NumPy MKL across sizes:
| N | ferray GFLOPs | %1cpeak | NumPy MKL | gap |
| 64 | 53 | 59% | 60 | 1.1x |
| 128 | 49 | 55% | 159 | 3.2x |
| 256 | 69 | 77% | 383 | 5.6x |
| 384 | 99 | 110% | 508 | 5.2x |
| 512 | 156 | 174% | 599 | 3.8x |

**Accuracy (statistical_equivalence.py: 47/47 PASS)**: unchanged. matmul 10x10 max ULP 104 → 81 (improved); 100x100 11264 (unchanged); ufuncs/stats all within thresholds.

Speed benchmark spot-check unchanged: matmul 10x10 6x faster, 50x50 1.2x slower, 100x100 1.5x slower (was 1.6x — marginal gain).

Remaining multi-thread gap to MKL (3.8-5.6x at N>=128): MKL uses all 28 logical cores via NUMA-aware coordination + B-prepack shared across threads. Our 8-thread cap is correct for hybrid Intel (avoids E-core penalty) but leaves throughput on the table that MKL extracts via OS scheduler tricks.
